name: npm Audit and Dependency Security
description: Scan Node.js dependencies for known vulnerabilities using npm audit and integrate security checks into your workflow.
trendingReason: Supply chain attacks on npm packages are increasing, making automated dependency security scanning essential for all projects.

steps.title: Run npm audit to scan dependencies
steps.description: Scan all installed dependencies against the npm advisory database for known vulnerabilities.
steps.tip: npm audit checks both direct and transitive dependencies. Run it regularly, not just during installs.

steps.title: Fix vulnerabilities automatically
steps.description: Let npm attempt to resolve vulnerabilities by updating to patched versions within your semver range.
steps.warning: The --force flag can introduce breaking changes by updating major versions. Avoid it unless you can test thoroughly.

steps.title: Review detailed vulnerability information
steps.description: Get a JSON report for programmatic analysis or detailed review of each vulnerability.
steps.command: npm audit --json | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin','utf8'); const r=JSON.parse(d); console.log('Total:', r.metadata.vulnerabilities.total); console.log('Critical:', r.metadata.vulnerabilities.critical); console.log('High:', r.metadata.vulnerabilities.high);"

steps.title: Add audit to CI pipeline
steps.description: Fail your CI build if high or critical vulnerabilities are found in dependencies.
steps.tip: Use --audit-level to set the minimum severity that causes a non-zero exit code: low, moderate, high, or critical.

steps.title: Check for outdated packages
steps.description: List packages that have newer versions available, as outdated packages often have unpatched vulnerabilities.

faq.question: What if npm audit fix does not resolve a vulnerability?
faq.answer: Some fixes require major version bumps (use --force cautiously) or the maintainer has not released a patch yet. You can use npm overrides in package.json to force a specific transitive dependency version, or find an alternative package.

faq.question: Should I fail CI on moderate vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: Start with --audit-level=high to avoid blocking on every moderate issue. Gradually tighten to moderate as you resolve existing vulnerabilities. Critical and high should always fail the build.

faq.question: Are dev dependencies a security risk?
faq.answer: Yes, during development and CI. A compromised dev dependency can inject malicious code during the build process. Audit all dependencies, not just production ones.
